concepts.name: Huffman Coding
concepts.description: An optimal data compression algorithm that uses a greedy approach to assign variable-length codes to input characters based on their frequencies.

concepts.name: Greedy Algorithm
concepts.description: An algorithmic paradigm that makes the locally optimal choice at each stage with the hope of finding a global optimum.

concepts.name: Data Compression
concepts.description: The process of encoding information using fewer bits than the original representation to reduce storage space or transmission bandwidth.

concepts.name: Data Encoding
concepts.description: The process of converting data from one format to another, typically for transmission, storage, or security.

concepts.name: Binary Representation
concepts.description: Representing data using only two symbols, typically 0 and 1, which is fundamental to how computers store and process information.

concepts.name: Fixed-Length Encoding
concepts.description: A coding scheme where every character or symbol is represented by a code of the same predefined bit length.

concepts.name: Character Frequencies
concepts.description: The count of occurrences for each unique character within a given message or data set, used as input for Huffman Coding.

concepts.name: Huffman Tree Construction
concepts.description: The step-by-step process of building a binary tree by repeatedly merging the two nodes with the smallest frequencies until a single root node remains.

concepts.name: Huffman Tree
concepts.description: A specially constructed binary tree where leaf nodes represent characters, internal nodes represent merged frequencies, and paths from the root to leaves define Huffman codes.

concepts.name: Assigning Binary Codes to Edges
concepts.description: The process of labeling the left edges of the Huffman tree with '0' and right edges with '1' to derive the unique binary code for each character.

concepts.name: Huffman Codes
concepts.description: Variable-length binary codes generated from a Huffman tree, where more frequent characters receive shorter codes and less frequent ones receive longer codes.

concepts.name: Prefix Codes
concepts.description: A type of code where no code word is a prefix of any other code word, ensuring unambiguous decoding, a property of Huffman codes.

concepts.name: Total Bits for Encoded Message
concepts.description: The sum of (frequency of each character × length of its Huffman code), representing the total size of the compressed message in bits.

concepts.name: Average Code Length
concepts.description: A metric calculated by dividing the total bits for the encoded message by the total number of characters, indicating the average number of bits per character.

concepts.name: Benefits of Huffman Coding
concepts.description: Advantages such as reduced data size, faster data transmission, and efficient storage compared to fixed-length encoding schemes.

prerequisites.rationale: Huffman Coding is explicitly introduced as an application of the Greedy Algorithm.
Data Compression is the primary goal and application explained for Huffman Coding.
Binary Representation is the fundamental concept underlying all digital Data Encoding for computers.
Fixed-Length Encoding is discussed as a less efficient alternative to highlight the advantages of Huffman Coding.
Character Frequencies are the essential input required to perform Huffman Tree Construction.
The Huffman Tree Construction algorithm directly results in the formation of the Huffman Tree data structure.
The Huffman Tree must be built before binary codes can be assigned to its edges to derive character codes.
Assigning binary codes to the edges of the Huffman Tree is the step that generates the actual Huffman Codes for characters.
Understanding Huffman Codes is necessary to comprehend their inherent property of being Prefix Codes.
The individual Huffman Codes and their lengths are used to calculate the Total Bits for the Encoded Message.
The Total Bits for the Encoded Message, along with total characters (derived from frequencies), are essential for calculating the Average Code Length.
Character Frequencies provide the total number of characters, which is needed to calculate the Average Code Length.
Understanding Huffman Coding itself is necessary to grasp its various Benefits.
